title: Introduction to HTML
summary: Document structure, elements, attributes, and semantic markup.

guide.title: Before you start — set up on your computer
guide.intro: Do these steps once before Example 1: Basic page skeleton. You will save your own HTML file on the Desktop and open it in a browser.

steps.title: Create a folder on your Desktop.
steps.text: Right-click on an empty area of your Desktop → New → Folder. Name it something easy to find, for example My Website.

steps.title: Create an HTML file inside the folder.
steps.text: Open the folder. Right-click inside → New → Text Document. Rename the file to index.html (type the full name including .html). If Windows asks “Are you sure you want to change the extension?” click Yes.

steps.title: Open the file in your browser.
steps.text: Double-click index.html. It may open as a blank page — that is normal for now.

steps.title: Open the same file in a text editor.
steps.text: Right-click index.html → Open with → Notepad (or use VS Code if you have it). Keep the editor open so you can paste code from the examples below.

steps.title: Get ready for Example 1.
steps.text: Scroll down to Basic page skeleton. Read the example, then build a complete page in your index.html — start with <!DOCTYPE html>, add <html>, <head>, and <body>, and put the example content inside <body>. Save the file (Ctrl+S), then refresh the browser tab to see your page.

guide.footer: When your folder, index.html, and text editor are ready, continue to Example 1: Basic page skeleton below.

examples.title: Basic page skeleton
examples.description: Minimum valid HTML5 document with head and body.

files.html:
Hello, HTML!

This is a paragraph inside the body.
files.css:
body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; padding: 1.5rem; max-width: 40rem; margin: 0 auto; }
h1 { color: #2563eb; }

examples.title: Headings and lists
examples.description: Heading levels h1–h3 and an unordered list.

examples.title: Page sections with div and landmarks
examples.description: Purpose: Real websites split content into header, navigation, main, and footer so visitors and search engines understand the page. Output: A simple page with a site title, nav links, welcome text, and a copyright footer. Try: Add a new link in the nav or a second paragraph inside main.

files.html:
My Site

Home | About | Contact

Welcome

This is the main content area where your page body lives.

© 2026 My Site

practice.description: Create a valid page with one h1, a short paragraph, and a link to https://developer.mozilla.org/

starter.html:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <title>My Page</title>
</head>
<body>
  <!-- Add h1, p, and a link here -->
</body>
</html>
